feedback.text: So I personally find this a step in the direction of digital consumer protection and, from my perspective, Cybersecurity. In addition, I deeply hope that the Stop Killing Games Initiave will gain more recognition and will be better perceived. The aim of this initiative is. Maintain video games even if they are no longer supported by publishers or developers. At the moment, what the gaming industry is permitted to do recently (worldwide) is not only unfair from the consumer’s point of view, but also criminal. When you buy a game, both physical and digital, we, as a consumer, should still have the right to own or to play. As Reinhold Schlager suggested on this page, ‘Ubisoft’ stopped her game ‘The Crew’ and the servers went offline, which became completely unplayable for all players holding this medium. https://stopkillinggames.wiki.gg/wiki/Dead_game_list The source indicated allows you to see the state of the threatened or dead games. Finally, Ubisoft tried to cover up in an update of her licensing. But what is at stake is not only to preserve old games, but generally to keep new and old games. Another example would be Nintendo itself. Apart from the new management there and the prizes, I find it more than behind-list when you buy a game and the console “Switch 2”: 1. You don't really have the game, and this can only be played using the internet and an access code. 2. The console can be ‘embroidered’. This should mean that all internet access is blocked by switch 2. If the lock is activated, it is of course possible to contact the support, but it will not be possible to unlock it until a longer period of time has passed. And this means that Nintendo can have a functional internet access. In order to come to the point, I find the new call for evidence very good and hopefully to improve a step in the right direction between consumers and the digital/media/gaming industry. Here are a Parr Source for Nintendo https://www.fr.de/ratgeber/games/switch-nintendo-bannt-account-wegen-gebrauchter-spiele-zr-93834892.html https://www.giga.de/games/nintendo-uebertreibt-voellig-switch-2-spieler-bangen-um-ihre-konsolen--01K04FP56VDQTE5FFQ3QCSB9H6 A further source to Ubisoft and The Crew: https://www.pcgamer.com/games/racing/ubisoft-is-stripping-peoples-licences-for-the-crew-weeks-after-its-shutdown-nearly-squandering-hopes-of-private-servers-and-acting-as-a-stark-reminder-of-how-volatile-digital-ownership-is/ and finally links to Stop Killing Game Movement: https://www.stopkillinggames.com https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HIfRLujXtUo&t=2727s
